source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Consider a power system shown in the <image>. Given that \( V_{S1} = V_{S2} = 1 + j0 \, \text{p.u} \); The positive sequence impedance are \( Z_{S1} = Z_{S2} = 0.001 + j0.01 \, \text{p.u} \) and \( Z_L = 0.006 + j0.06 \, \text{p.u} \). 3-phase Base MVA = 100, voltage base = 400 kV (Line to Line), Nominal system frequency = 50 Hz. The reference voltage for phase 'a' is defined as \( V(t) = V_m \cos(\omega t) \). A symmetrical three-phase fault occurs at the center of the line, i.e., point 'F' at time \( t_0 \). The positive sequence impedance from source S1 to point 'F' equals \( 0.004 + j0.04 \, \text{p.u} \). The waveform corresponding to phase 'a' fault current from bus X reveals that decaying d.c. offset current is negative and in magnitude at its maximum initial value. Assume that the negative sequence impedances are equal to positive sequence impedance and the zero sequence impedances are three times positive sequence impedances.
The rms value of the component of fault current (\( I_x \)) will be ________
extraction_notes: solution is an option letter and was mapped using inline choices in the problem text
image field contains an in-memory PIL image placeholder, not a path or URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the figure'
the topology/location information (bus X, point F, line center, waveform cue) appears tied to the figure rather than fully self-contained in text
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires combining network impedance data, fault condition, and fault location information
the statement about decaying d.c. offset polarity and maximum initial magnitude adds an additional inference beyond direct substitution
the problem involves per-unit quantities plus conversion to physical current units (kA)
multiple provided parameters and 4 answer choices indicate a nontrivial engineering calculation
verifiability: answer_text is a specific numeric result with unit: '3.59 kA'
answer_type=short_text and choices are discrete numeric options, enabling exact or near-exact checking
given impedances, bases, and fault description, the computation path is in principle checkable by a rule-based or symbolic verifier

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantic assets are available
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
although some figure-dependent details are not fully transcribed in text, the referenced figure exists, so the sample remains recoverable and usable
noted risks such as moderate image readability and answer unit normalization appear manageable rather than disqualifying

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
Consider a power system shown in the figure. Given that \(V_{S1} = V_{S2} = 1 + j0\,\text{p.u.}\); the positive-sequence impedances are \(Z_{S1} = Z_{S2} = 0.001 + j0.01\,\text{p.u.}\) and \(Z_L = 0.006 + j0.06\,\text{p.u.}\). 3-phase base MVA = 100, voltage base = 400 kV (line-to-line), nominal system frequency = 50 Hz. The reference voltage for phase 'a' is defined as \(V(t) = V_m \cos(\omega t)\). A symmetrical three-phase fault occurs at the center of the line, i.e., point 'F', at time \(t_0\). The positive-sequence impedance from source S1 to point 'F' equals \(0.004 + j0.04\,\text{p.u.}\). The waveform corresponding to phase 'a' fault current from bus X reveals that the decaying d.c. offset current is negative and has its maximum initial magnitude. Assume that the negative-sequence impedances are equal to the positive-sequence impedances and the zero-sequence impedances are three times the positive-sequence impedances.
The rms value of the component of fault current (\(I_x\)) will be ________.

agent_assessment.rationale: The question text fully specifies the fault-analysis setup and expected quantity, and the diagram is simple but clear enough to confirm the two-source line, midpoint fault F, buses X and Y, and current Ix direction. Despite modest image quality, the image-text pair is semantically understandable.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample is semantically complete and solvable, with a usable rewrite and a complete answer signal. However, the alignment record is marked risky and the image has crop-integrity/readability concerns, so it retains recoverable value but should be checked before passing.

conflict_pairs.detail: 文本中的视觉锚点明显多于可识别视觉区域。

field_audit_records.before_value:
Consider a power system shown in the <image>. Given that \( V_{S1} = V_{S2} = 1 + j0 \, \text{p.u} \); The positive sequence impedance are \( Z_{S1} = Z_{S2} = 0.001 + j0.01 \, \text{p.u} \) and \( Z_L = 0.006 + j0.06 \, \text{p.u} \). 3-phase Base MVA = 100, voltage base = 400 kV (Line to Line), Nominal system frequency = 50 Hz. The reference voltage for phase 'a' is defined as \( V(t) = V_m \cos(\omega t) \). A symmetrical three-phase fault occurs at the center of the line, i.e., point 'F' at time \( t_0 \). The positive sequence impedance from source S1 to point 'F' equals \( 0.004 + j0.04 \, \text{p.u} \). The waveform corresponding to phase 'a' fault current from bus X reveals that decaying d.c. offset current is negative and in magnitude at its maximum initial value. Assume that the negative sequence impedances are equal to positive sequence impedance and the zero sequence impedances are three times positive sequence impedances.
The rms value of the component of fault current (\( I_x \)) will be ________
field_audit_records.after_value:
Consider a power system shown in the figure. Given that \(V_{S1} = V_{S2} = 1 + j0\,\text{p.u.}\); the positive-sequence impedances are \(Z_{S1} = Z_{S2} = 0.001 + j0.01\,\text{p.u.}\) and \(Z_L = 0.006 + j0.06\,\text{p.u.}\). 3-phase base MVA = 100, voltage base = 400 kV (line-to-line), nominal system frequency = 50 Hz. The reference voltage for phase 'a' is defined as \(V(t) = V_m \cos(\omega t)\). A symmetrical three-phase fault occurs at the center of the line, i.e., point 'F', at time \(t_0\). The positive-sequence impedance from source S1 to point 'F' equals \(0.004 + j0.04\,\text{p.u.}\). The waveform corresponding to phase 'a' fault current from bus X reveals that the decaying d.c. offset current is negative and has its maximum initial magnitude. Assume that the negative-sequence impedances are equal to the positive-sequence impedances and the zero-sequence impedances are three times the positive-sequence impedances.
The rms value of the component of fault current (\(I_x\)) will be ________.

rewrite_reports.rationale: The stem is already an open-ended fill-in-the-blank question; removing the multiple-choice options preserves the original task semantics.

variants.title: Dataset Open Question
variants.rewritten_question_text:
Consider a power system shown in the figure. Given that \(V_{S1} = V_{S2} = 1 + j0\,\text{p.u.}\); the positive-sequence impedances are \(Z_{S1} = Z_{S2} = 0.001 + j0.01\,\text{p.u.}\) and \(Z_L = 0.006 + j0.06\,\text{p.u.}\). 3-phase base MVA = 100, voltage base = 400 kV (line-to-line), nominal system frequency = 50 Hz. The reference voltage for phase 'a' is defined as \(V(t) = V_m \cos(\omega t)\). A symmetrical three-phase fault occurs at the center of the line, i.e., point 'F', at time \(t_0\). The positive-sequence impedance from source S1 to point 'F' equals \(0.004 + j0.04\,\text{p.u.}\). The waveform corresponding to phase 'a' fault current from bus X reveals that the decaying d.c. offset current is negative and has its maximum initial magnitude. Assume that the negative-sequence impedances are equal to the positive-sequence impedances and the zero-sequence impedances are three times the positive-sequence impedances.
The rms value of the component of fault current (\(I_x\)) will be ________.
